D11 ComputationalL3 ApplyReference

Bayer Ordered Dither — Threshold Matrix

Add a tiled threshold matrix before rounding; deterministic, parallelisable, no error propagation — used in 16-colour VGA, GameBoy LCDs, and pixel-art aesthetics

Bayer matrices are designed to maximise high-frequency separation between threshold values. 4×4 has 16 unique thresholds; 8×8 has 64. Result: regular crosshatch pattern, very different from FS noise. No state means trivial GPU shader implementation — and the classic look of 80s/90s computer graphics.

Toggle matrix size + levels.
